text: Feature 1: `updated_at` index missing — adding `since` on updated_at would cause full table scans; no corresponding index in migrations.ts
Feature 1: `memory_query` is a relevance search, not a change feed — `since` doesn't make 'what changed?' a first-class operation; wrong abstraction
Feature 1: Post-KNN date filtering distorts semantic/hybrid results — highly relevant recent entries invisible if ranked below KNN cutoff globally
Feature 2: `agent_id` hardcoded to 'default' in all tool handlers — caller-supplied agent_id is unverified and spoofable; identity model doesn't exist
Feature 2: HTTP layer auth context not passed into tool execution — prerequisite architectural change required before session-aware orient is viable
Feature 3: Attention partly already solved — maintenance suggestions already compute stale projects, missing lifecycle tags, blocked statuses; gap is narrower than claimed
Feature 4: Compact token stored in in-memory Map — not durable across restarts, not safe for stateless HTTP
Feature 4: Semantic drift between compact Phase 1 and Phase 2 — no binding of candidate set; new logs arriving between phases could be incorrectly pruned
Missing feature: `memory_history` — audit_log table exists with no MCP tool exposing it; it is the correct abstraction for 'what changed?' queries
Missing feature: caller context plumbing — tool handlers receive only db handle, not auth/session context; architectural prerequisite for multiple features
Claude quoted fictional audit_log schema with entry_id and content fields — actual schema (migrations.ts:44) has only id, timestamp, agent_id, action, namespace, key, detail
HMAC token fixes in-memory Map but doesn't solve candidate-set drift — 'latest_log_id still exists' check doesn't prove no newer logs arrived
memory_history and narrow lexical since risk overlapping/muddled surfaces — distinction must be explicit: history=mutation timeline, query+date=content search
Caller identity weakness is the same architectural problem for both memory_history attribution and orient diff — Claude treated it as blocking one but incidental to the other
